nodes.name: Transcript Webhook
nodes.notes: Receives a meeting transcript. POST body should include: transcript (string), client_name (string), meeting_type (string, e.g. 'discovery', 'review', 'scrum'), meeting_date (ISO string). Can be triggered manually from n8n UI by pasting a transcript.

parameters.name: prompt

nodes.name: Process with Ollama (Local)
nodes.notes: Sends transcript to Ollama running locally in Docker. Uses mistral by default — good balance of speed and quality for meeting processing. Swap to llama3 for larger/more complex transcripts. All processing stays on your VPS — transcript never leaves your server.

assignments.name: markdown_doc

nodes.name: Save Notes to MinIO
nodes.notes: Saves the processed meeting notes as a markdown file in the client's MinIO folder. Creates the folder automatically if it does not exist.

parameters.text:
=📋 *Meeting Processed*

👤 *{{ $('Extract Inputs').item.json.client_name }}*
📅 {{ $('Extract Inputs').item.json.meeting_type }} — {{ $('Extract Inputs').item.json.meeting_date }}

{{ $('Format Output').item.json.processed_output.substring(0, 800) }}...

📁 Full notes saved to MinIO: `clients/{{ $('Extract Inputs').item.json.file_slug }}/meeting-notes.md`

nodes.name: Send Summary to Telegram
nodes.notes: Sends the first 800 chars of the processed output to Telegram as a preview. Full notes are always saved to MinIO regardless.

meta.description: Takes a raw meeting transcript and uses local Ollama (mistral) to generate: a structured summary, decision list, action items checklist, ready-to-send follow-up email, and key client insights. Saves the full output as a markdown file in the client's MinIO folder and previews it via Telegram. All processing is local — transcript never leaves your server.
meta.chapter_reference: Part II, Chapter 9 — Running AI Locally
meta.setup_instructions: 1. Set TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID. 2. Add MinIO S3 or httpHeaderAuth credential. 3. Paste webhook URL into your meeting note workflow or trigger manually via n8n UI. 4. To use with Google Meet / Zoom: export transcript as text, paste into a POST request to this webhook. 5. To use with Whisper transcription: chain with a prior Whisper-transcribe workflow and pass the output as 'transcript' in the body.
